structuralIssues.title: Five content sections empty
structuralIssues.description: content.standards_landscape, content.standards_development, content.stakeholder_notes, content.key_risks, and content.sources_summary are empty.
structuralIssues.action_required: Populate with proposed content from deep-dive report §8.

structuralIssues.title: HUESKER and Naue publish geogrid EPDs but broader geosynthetics portfolio lacks EPD coverage
structuralIssues.description: HUESKER publishes EN 15804 EPDs for Fortrac® T geogrid (Kiwa-verified). Naue publishes EPDs for Secugrid® geogrids. The HUESKER Fortrac EPD was highlighted as the first geogrid EPD — a milestone for the industry. However, EPDs for geotextiles (filtration/separation), geomembranes (barrier), and geocomposites (drainage) were not found. IGS claims geosynthetics carbon footprint is typically 65% lower than alternative construction materials. For DPP: the geosynthetics industry is early in its EPD journey with reinforcement products leading, but the broader portfolio (8 of 10 application-specific hENs) lacks environmental data infrastructure.
structuralIssues.source: HUESKER EPD via Kiwa (https://www.kiwa.com/globalassets/germany/veroffentlichte-epds/06-0921/epd-huesker-134-en.pdf/); Naue EPDs (https://www.naue.com/info-center/downloads/environmental-product-declaration/); Geosynthetica (https://www.geosynthetica.com/geogrid-earns-environmental-product-declaration/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document EPD readiness gap in content. Note geogrid-only coverage vs 10 application-specific hENs.

structuralIssues.title: ECHA PFAS ban creates dual pressure on geomembranes — products contain PFAS but manufacturing may use PFAS aids
structuralIssues.description: ECHA proposed universal PFAS restriction (Feb 2023) creates dual regulatory pressure on geomembranes: HDPE geomembranes are critical infrastructure for containing PFAS-laden leachate in landfills (intact membranes contain PFAS 30+ years, permeability <10⁻¹⁴ cm/s), but some geomembrane manufacturing processes use PFAS-containing processing aids. Manufacturers developing PFAS-free products. Additionally, EU investigating microplastics release from geotextiles — IGS formally responded advocating science-based evidence. For DPP: EN 13257 (solid waste) and EN 13265 (liquid waste) products may need to declare both PFAS containment performance AND PFAS-free manufacturing status.
structuralIssues.source: ScienceDirect PFAS permeation (https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0269749124019511); EPI PFAS containment (https://www.geomembrane.com/about/blog/pfas-containment-why-liners-are-now-more-important-than-ever.html); Nonwovens News microplastics (https://www.nonwovensnews.com/manufacturing-news/15928-eu-investigating-release-of-microplastics-from-geotextiles)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document PFAS dual-pressure and microplastics regulatory risk in content.key_risks. Flag as cross-regulatory DPP data challenge.

structuralIssues.title: European geosynthetics market $2.94B (2025) — geotextiles 38%, Germany 23%, circular economy emerging
structuralIssues.description: European geosynthetics market $2.94B (2025), projected $4.32B by 2034 (CAGR 4.36%). Geotextiles 38.2% of market. GCLs fastest-growing segment (9.3% CAGR). Germany leads 23.3%, France 16.3%. Construction sector produces 850M+ tonnes waste annually with geosynthetics underrepresented in circular economy. Netherlands project recycled 500,000 m² geotextiles (EuroGeo8 2025). 100% recycled PP composite geogrids developed (FHWA-documented). Economic barriers remain: soil contamination in recovered geotextiles, cost disparities with virgin material.
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market context and circular economy developments in content.
